title: The First Resistance Against the Mongol-Yuan
summary: The world's most powerful Mongol-Yuan invaders tasted their first defeat in Dai Viet in 1258.

slides.text: Did you know? In the 13th century, there was a terrifying army called the Mongol-Yuan that had conquered many countries in the world.
slides.imagePrompt: Map of the world with a huge red area (Mongol Empire) spreading, shadows over other lands, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When they invaded our country, the Tran King and court used the 'Scorched Earth' (Vuon khong nha trong) strategy - withdrawing all people and food from the capital.
slides.imagePrompt: Empty ancient streets of Thang Long, houses with doors closed, no one around, silence, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The enemy entered the city with nothing to eat and was constantly harassed by our attacks. They became extremely tired and hungry.
slides.imagePrompt: Mongol soldiers looking tired and hungry in an empty city, rain falling, campfire with no food, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: In 1258, at Dong Bo Dau, our troops launched a counter-attack and crushed the Mongol-Yuan army, forcing them to flee back to their country in humiliation.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese cavalry charging at the retreating Mongol soldiers, banners of the Tran dynasty flying high, victory, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What is the famous strategy of the Tran Dynasty when fighting the enemy?
Where did the largest victory of the first resistance take place?
